rulings.Case Title: BURT HOWARD ET AL VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 16, 2016, Tuesday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Cambridge Financial Management, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is overruled in its entirety. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit A and denied as to Exhibit B. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment to complaint fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action based on the time period identified. If the Court granted defendant's request for judicial notice of Exhibit B, the Court would only take judicial notice of the existence of the Business Entity Detail, not the truth of the facts stated within. Finally, even if the Court took judicial notice of the fact that Cambridge Financial Management, Inc. filed in 1983, it still would not demonstrate that plaintiffs fail to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against defendant. Defendant's demurrer on the ground of uncertainty is improper and/or not sufficiently specific. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' allegations are insufficient pursuant to Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' claims are barred by Workers' Compensation exclusivity. Based on the above, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff's loss of consortium cause of action fails. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
